CDISC Definition: Terminology related to the non-clinical cardiovascular test code codelist.
NCI Preferred Term: CDISC SEND Cardiovascular Test Code Terminology

CDISC Definition: A measurement of a subject's physical activity or movement.
A relative measurement (ratio) of the maximum rate of pressure rise (dP/dt maximum) to maximum isovolumetric pressure.
The amount of time it takes for a capillary bed to refill with blood after pressure blanching.
The minimum blood pressure in the systemic arterial circulation during the cardiac cycle.

CDISC Synonym(s): Left Ventricular Positive dP/dt 40mmHg; Left Ventricular Positive dP/dt at 40mmHg; Positive dP/dt at 40mmHg; Positive dP/dt Derivative at 40mmHg
CDISC Definition: The maximum rate of positive pressure change over time within the left ventricle (systole) at 40mmHg.
The average rate of pressure change over time within the left ventricle (systole).

CDISC Synonym(s): +dPdt; dPdt Maximum; Left Ventricular Maximum Positive dP/dt
CDISC Definition: The maximum rate of positive pressure change over time within the left ventricle (systole).

CDISC Synonym(s): -dP/dt; Left Ventricular Maximum Negative dP/dt; Left Ventricular Minimum Positive dP/dt
CDISC Definition: The minimum rate of positive pressure change over time within the left ventricle (systole).
The average rate of negative pressure change over time within the left ventricle (systole).
The number of heartbeats per unit of time, usually expressed as beats per minute. (NCI)
The pressure within the left ventricle of the heart at the end of the period when the chambers of the heart refill with blood and just before the contraction begins.
The pressure within the left ventricle of the heart during the contraction of the left ventricle of the heart.
The mean pressure of the blood within the arterial circulation.

CDISC Synonym(s): Left Ventricular Pressure Maximum; Pressure at dP/dt Maximum
CDISC Definition: The positive pressure that correlates with the maximum dP/dt, the rate of change in pressure over time.

CDISC Synonym(s): Left Ventricular Pressure Minimum; Pressure at dP/dt Minimum
CDISC Definition: The positive pressure that correlates with the minimum dP/dt, the rate of change in pressure over time.
The change in systolic to diastolic pressure which produces a pulse.
The maximum blood pressure in the systemic arterial circulation during the cardiac cycle.

CDISC Synonym(s): Systemic Vascular Resistance; Total Peripheral Resistance
CDISC Definition: The resistance to blood flow through the systemic vasculature.
